steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is the gerund phrase "Having proportionally large populations." A gerund phrase acting as a subject is grammatically singular, so it requires a singular verb.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have been': plural verb — does not agree with a singular gerund subject. ✗
  • Choice B — 'is': singular present-tense verb — correctly agrees with the gerund phrase subject. ✓
  • Choice C — 'were': plural past-tense verb — fails subject-verb agreement on both number and tense. ✗
  • Choice D — 'are': plural verb — does not agree with a singular gerund subject. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
